message:

@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k3s-5618-k3s-as-rpms",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k3s: k3s as RPMs",
+    "description": "k3s as RPMs. Requested by 15+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k3s deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k3s version and configuration:\n```bash\nk3s --version\nkubectl get nodes\n```\nThis feature requires a working k3s installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k3s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:\n```bash\nk3s --version\nkubectl get nodes -o wide\nkubectl get pods -A\n```\nAt the moment is is not possible to build k3s as an RPM or DEB package. This means I have to use the curl installer to install k3s on my host system."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for k3s as RPMs",
+        "description": "#### Proposed Changes ####\n\nCurrently it is not possible to build k3s from source due to the build scripts mixing the stages of download and build.\n\n#### Types of Changes ####\n\nI would consider this a bugfix as this was possible in the past.\n\n#### Verification ####\n\nThe build in the OBS (which\n```yaml\nOkay I tried digging further into the build failure but I don't find a clue here to continue sadly. Help would be much appreciated:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes -o wide\nkubectl get pods -A\nkubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"k3s as RPMs\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "#### Proposed Changes ####\n\nCurrently it is not possible to build k3s from source due to the build scripts mixing the stages of download and build.\n\n#### Types of Changes ####\n\nI would consider this a bugfix as this was possible in the past.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Okay I tried digging further into the build failure but I don't find a clue here to continue sadly. Help would be much appreciated:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
